visualGoal: Make an 8.5-foot-basement owner believe the room is buildable BEFORE they hit the honest compromises, and steer them hard toward side-of-ball photometric sensors and short-throw projection. Real launch-monitor and projector product proof throughout; overhead units appear only as the 'do not buy' text callout, never as body imagery.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Intentionally text-only in this article as the 'don't buy for a low ceiling' example (needs 9-10 ft). A verified photo would only matter for OTHER (high-ceiling) golf articles, not this one. Not a blocker for this article's certification.
Overhead unit requiring a 9'6" ceiling; text-only here as an avoid-for-low-ceilings mention. Not needed for this article; useful only for a dedicated-room/overhead build article.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic finished 8.5-foot-ceiling basement golf bay at warm evening light, exposed duct and joist run clearly low over one corner, impact screen glowing with a par-3, a small side-of-ball launch monitor placed on the mat beside the tee with an open product-overlay footprint for a real SkyTrak+ photo, short-throw projector mounted flush near the screen ahead of the hitting position, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, overhead ceiling-mounted camera unit, cathedral ceiling, warped clubs, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: What each ceiling height actually buys you
content: Under 8.0 ft: wedges & short irons only, driver off the table
8.0-8.5 ft: irons & fairway woods, driver for shorter/flatter swings (real-world minimum)
9.0 ft: full bag for most, floor-unit manufacturer minimum
10+ ft: full bag all heights, overhead sensors on the table
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable text, golf greens/gold, a swing-arc silhouette rising into each ceiling tier

infographicPrompts.title: Which launch monitor tech fits a low ceiling
content: Side-of-ball photometric = CORRECT (no overhead clearance): SkyTrak+, Launch Pro, R50, Square Golf
Overhead camera = WRONG (needs 9-10 ft): Uneekor EYE XO2, Foresight Falcon
Radar = USABLE WITH CARE (needs 14-16 ft depth behind hitter): Garmin R10
infographicPrompts.style: Three-lane traffic-light decision card (green/red/amber), real product-photo placeholders as row markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Short-throw vs ultra-short-throw for low rooms
content: Long-throw behind golfer = shadow + steals overhead space (avoid)
Short-throw 0.4-1.0 ratio, ceiling-mount near screen, ~13-14 ft throw
Ultra-short-throw under 0.4, floor shield 4-5 ft from screen (rooms under 8.5 ft)
Any floor unit MUST be inside a protective enclosure
infographicPrompts.style: Side-view room cutaway showing swing arc and light path for each option, Optoma GT2100HDR photo placeholder as the short-throw marker

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult golf curator Nina Brooks in a low-ceiling basement bay, mid slow-motion driver backswing, one eye on the clubhead near the ceiling as she demonstrates the driver-clearance test, calm instructor energy, tape measure visible on the mat, product-overlay space beside her, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.
